name: Swedish Rye Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 Teaspoon Orange Rind, Grated and Sifted
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
2. Dissolve the yeast in the warm (105-115 degrees) water in a large mixing bowl.
3. Let the yeast rest for 3 to 5 minutes.
4. Add the molasses, sugar, salt, grated orange rind, fennel seed, anise seed, and chopped raisins.
5. Stir in the rye flour and the softened butter.
6. Beat these ingredients together until smooth.
7. Add the sifted all-purpose flour.
8. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board.
9. Knead until the dough is smooth, elastic, and satiny (about 10 minutes or so), adding flour as needed to prevent sticking and improve texture.
10. Grease a large bowl and turn the dough into it.
11. Turn the dough in the bowl so that all sides are covered with grease.
12. Cover the bowl and allow the dough to rise until doubled in bulk.
13. Turn the dough out onto a baking sheet lightly floured with cornmeal.
14. Punch the dough down and divide into equal parts.
15. Form each part into an oval and cover with a cloth.
16. Allow to rise until almost doubled in bulk.
17. Make four 1/4-inch deep diagonal slashes on the tops of the loaves.
18. Bake until tapping on the bottom produces a hollow sound (30-35 minutes).
19. Remove from the baking sheet and cool on a wire rack.
20. Allow the bread to cool completely before wrapping.
